description: Notification System — Persistent in-app notifications (bell + panel), toast notifications, email notifications with preferences, and notification service that consumes events from RabbitMQ and dispatches to users

userStories.title: Notification REST API
userStories.description: As a user, I want to list my persistent notifications, see unread count, and mark notifications as read/acted, so that I can stay informed of important events
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/notifications returns paginated list with unread_count
Supports unread_only=true query param to filter unread notifications
GET /api/notifications/unread-count returns badge count
PATCH /api/notifications/:id marks is_read or action_taken
POST /api/notifications/mark-all-read updates all user notifications
Returns 404 if notification does not exist or does not belong to user
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § Notifications — list, unread-count, mark read/acted endpoints
Design: Component inventory mentions NotificationBell and NotificationPanel but no specific specs in component-specs.md
Testing: docs/04-test-architecture/test-matrix.md § API-NOTIF.01-04
Files: services/gateway/apps/notifications/views.py (new), services/gateway/apps/notifications/urls.py (new), services/gateway/gateway/urls.py (mount notifications app), services/gateway/apps/notifications/tests/test_notification_api.py (new)
Gotchas: Notification model already exists in services/gateway/apps/notifications/models.py — use it directly. Filter by user_id and apply pagination. Notifications are owned by gateway (not core).

data_model: notifications: id uuid PK, user_id uuid FK, event_type varchar(50), title varchar(300), body text, reference_id uuid, reference_type varchar(30), is_read bool, action_taken bool, created_at timestamptz
test_cases: API-NOTIF.01 | GET /api/notifications | Happy path | 200 + paginated list

userStories.title: Notification Creation Service (Gateway gRPC)
userStories.description: As the notification service, I want to create notification records via gateway gRPC, so that persistent notifications are stored in the database
acceptanceCriteria: Gateway exposes CreateNotification gRPC method
Accepts user_id, event_type, title, body, reference_id, reference_type
Creates Notification row in database
Returns notification_id on success
Notification service can call this via gRPC client
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § Gateway Service gRPC — CreateNotification method. proto/gateway.proto defines the CreateNotificationRequest/Response messages.
Design: No design component — this is backend gRPC service.
Files: proto/gateway.proto (CreateNotification already defined), services/gateway/gateway_service.py (implement CreateNotification servicer method), services/gateway/apps/notifications/models.py (use existing Notification model)
Gotchas: Gateway gRPC service pattern already established in M1-M11. Use database_sync_to_async for ORM operations. Notification model is unmanaged in core but managed in gateway — gateway owns the table.

data_model: notifications: id uuid PK, user_id uuid FK, event_type varchar(50), title varchar(300), body text, reference_id uuid, reference_type varchar(30), is_read bool default false, action_taken bool default false, created_at timestamptz
existing_code: services/gateway/gateway_service.py: existing gRPC servicers for GetUserPreferences, GetAlertRecipients — add CreateNotification

userStories.title: Notification Service Consumer — Event Processing & Email Dispatch
userStories.description: As the notification service, I want to consume notification events from RabbitMQ, create persistent notifications via gateway gRPC, and dispatch email notifications respecting user preferences, so that users are notified of important events
acceptanceCriteria: Notification service consumes events from collaboration, review, chat, AI queues
For each event: creates notification via gateway CreateNotification gRPC
Fetches user email preferences via gateway GetUserPreferences gRPC
Sends email if preference is enabled for that event type
Email includes event details, link to relevant resource, unsubscribe hint
Handles missing user preferences gracefully (default to enabled)
Service runs as standalone consumer (services/notification/main.py entry point)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/project-structure.md § Notification Service — stateless consumer, consumes events from RabbitMQ, calls gateway gRPC for notification creation and user preferences. Email dispatch via SMTP (no dedicated email service yet — use Python smtplib or similar).
Design: No design component — backend service.
Files: services/notification/main.py (consumer entry point), services/notification/consumers/base.py, services/notification/consumers/collaboration_events.py (new), services/notification/consumers/review_events.py (existing), services/notification/consumers/ai_events.py (existing), services/notification/email/sender.py (new), services/notification/grpc_clients/gateway_client.py (CreateNotification, GetUserPreferences)
Gotchas: Notification service already has structure for consumers and gRPC clients from previous milestones. Reuse existing patterns. Email sender should be a shared module. Email templates can be simple string formatting for MVP — no Jinja2 templates yet. Events are published to RabbitMQ by core/gateway services (not implemented yet — this story focuses on the consumer side).
Deviations: Event publisher integration (core/gateway) is deferred to US-005 (wire all events). This story implements the consumer side only.

data_model: users: email_notification_preferences jsonb — per-event-type toggles, empty = all enabled

userStories.title: Email Notification Preferences API
userStories.description: As a user, I want to view and update my email notification preferences, so that I can control which event types trigger email delivery
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/users/me/notification-preferences returns categorized preferences
Categories: Collaboration, Review, Chat, Similarity, Admin (if admin role)
Missing preference keys default to true (all enabled by default)
PATCH /api/users/me/notification-preferences updates preferences (partial merge)
Gateway gRPC GetUserPreferences method fetches preferences for notification service
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § User endpoints — GET/PATCH notification-preferences. Users table has email_notification_preferences jsonb column.
Design: docs/03-design/component-inventory.md § EmailPreferencesPanel — grouped toggles, role-based sections.
Testing: docs/04-test-architecture/test-matrix.md § API-USER.02-03
Files: services/gateway/apps/authentication/views.py (add notification-preferences endpoints), services/gateway/apps/authentication/user_urls.py (mount routes), services/gateway/apps/authentication/tests/test_notification_prefs.py (new), services/gateway/gateway_service.py (implement GetUserPreferences gRPC)
Gotchas: Preferences are stored as jsonb on users table. Use JSONB field merge pattern for PATCH. Gateway already has GetUserPreferences gRPC stub from proto/gateway.proto — implement it. Default to all enabled (absence = enabled).

data_model: users: email_notification_preferences jsonb — per-type toggles, empty = all enabled
api_endpoints: gRPC GatewayService.GetUserPreferences — fetches prefs for notification service

userStories.title: Wire All Notification Events — Collaboration, Review, Chat, AI
userStories.description: As the system, I want to publish notification events to RabbitMQ when collaboration invitations are sent/accepted/declined, review state changes, users are @mentioned in chat, and AI delegation completes, so that users receive timely notifications
acceptanceCriteria: Collaboration events: invitation sent, accepted, declined, collaborator joined, left, removed, ownership transferred
Review events: idea submitted, state changed (accepted/dropped/rejected/undone), comment posted
Chat events: @mention detected in chat message
AI events: delegation complete (AI responds after delegation message)
Events published to RabbitMQ exchange with routing keys
Event payload includes user_id (recipient), event_type, title, body, reference_id, reference_type
Notifications are created by notification service consumer (US-003)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § WebSocket Protocol and features.md § FA-12.5 — All Notification Events table. RabbitMQ event publishing pattern from M6 WebSocket milestone.
Design: No design component — backend event publishing.
Testing: No test matrix entries — integration testing via notification service consumer.
Files: services/gateway/apps/collaboration/views.py (publish events on invite/accept/decline/remove/transfer), services/gateway/apps/review/views.py (publish on submit/accept/reject/drop/undo/comment), services/gateway/apps/ideas/views.py (publish on chat @mention detection), services/core/tasks.py or ai service (publish AI delegation complete), common event publisher utility (services/gateway/utils/events.py or similar)
Gotchas: RabbitMQ connection and publishing pattern already established in M6. Reuse existing event bus setup. @mention detection requires parsing chat message content for @user[<uuid>] patterns. AI delegation complete event requires AI service coordination — may need to defer to AI milestone if not implemented yet.
Deviations: AI delegation complete event may require AI service changes — coordinate with AI Engineer or defer if AI service not ready. Focus on collaboration, review, and chat events first.

api_endpoints: RabbitMQ event publishing — collaboration, review, chat, AI events
existing_code: M6 WebSocket: RabbitMQ event publisher pattern
services/gateway/apps/review/views.py: review action endpoints — add event publishing

userStories.title: NotificationBell Component
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a notification bell icon in the navbar with an unread count badge, so that I can quickly see if I have new notifications
acceptanceCriteria: Bell icon (Lucide Bell) in navbar utility section
Unread count badge displays when count > 0
Badge shows count up to 99, displays '99+' for higher counts
Badge has scale-in animation on count increase
Clicking bell opens NotificationPanel (floating window)
Unread count fetched from GET /api/notifications/unread-count
Count updates in real-time via WebSocket notification events
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § GET /api/notifications/unread-count returns count.
Design: docs/03-design/component-specs.md § 3.3 Notification Count Badge — position, size, color, animation, max '99+' display. docs/03-design/component-inventory.md § NotificationBell in Layout Components.
Testing: No test matrix entry for frontend components — manual verification.
Files: frontend/src/components/layout/NotificationBell.tsx (new), frontend/src/components/layout/Navbar.tsx (add bell to utility section), frontend/src/api/notifications.ts (new — API client), frontend/src/__tests__/notification-bell.test.tsx (new)
Gotchas: NotificationBell is a layout component (navbar element), not a feature component. Badge animation uses CSS scale-in on count change. WebSocket notification event updates the count (listen for 'notification' type events). Use TanStack Query for API state management. Bell click toggles NotificationPanel visibility (shared state via React context or local state).

components: NotificationBell — bell icon + badge, Layout component in navbar
Navbar — add NotificationBell to utility section

userStories.title: NotificationPanel Component
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a floating panel with my persistent notifications when I click the bell, so that I can view notification details and take actions
acceptanceCriteria: Floating panel anchored to notification bell (similar to IdeasListFloating pattern)
Lists notifications with icon, title, body, timestamp
Each notification shows inline action buttons if applicable (e.g., accept/decline invitation)
Clicking a notification item navigates to relevant context (idea workspace, review page)
Clicking navigation marks notification as action_taken
Panel shows empty state when no notifications exist
Panel fetches notifications from GET /api/notifications
Real-time updates: new notifications appear via WebSocket
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § GET /api/notifications returns list with pagination.
Design: docs/03-design/component-inventory.md § NotificationPanel and NotificationItem — icon + title + time + description + actions. docs/03-design/component-specs.md § 11.3 Empty States — 'All caught up' message.
Testing: No test matrix entry for frontend components — manual verification.
Files: frontend/src/components/notifications/NotificationPanel.tsx (new), frontend/src/components/notifications/NotificationItem.tsx (new), frontend/src/api/notifications.ts (add fetchNotifications, markNotificationRead), frontend/src/__tests__/notification-panel.test.tsx (new)
Gotchas: Floating panel pattern similar to IdeasListFloating (M2) — use shadcn Popover or Dialog. NotificationItem action buttons depend on event_type (invitation → accept/decline, merge request → view details, etc.). Navigation on click uses react-router navigate() and marks action_taken via API. WebSocket 'notification' event triggers refetch or optimistic insert. Empty state uses Lucide Check icon.

components: NotificationPanel — floating window with notification list
NotificationItem — icon + title + time + body + action buttons

userStories.title: WebSocket Notification Delivery — Real-Time Bell Update & Toast
userStories.description: As a user, I want to receive real-time notifications via WebSocket, so that I see toast alerts and the bell count updates immediately when events occur
acceptanceCriteria: WebSocket consumer listens for 'notification' events
On notification event: displays toast (react-toastify) with event title and body
Toast type matches event severity (info, success, warning)
Bell unread count increments immediately (optimistic update)
NotificationPanel refetches list to show new notification
WebSocket notification event is user-scoped (not idea-scoped)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § WebSocket Protocol — 'notification' event delivered to user regardless of current idea.
Design: docs/03-design/component-specs.md § 11.1 Toast Notifications — position, auto-dismiss, types.
Testing: No test matrix entry — manual verification.
Files: frontend/src/hooks/useWebSocket.ts (add 'notification' event handler), frontend/src/components/notifications/NotificationBell.tsx (update count on event), frontend/src/components/notifications/NotificationPanel.tsx (refetch on event), frontend/src/__tests__/websocket-notifications.test.tsx (new)
Gotchas: WebSocket notification event structure: {type: 'notification', payload: {id, event_type, title, body, reference_id, reference_type, created_at}}. Toast uses react-toastify (already configured). Optimistic count increment before API refetch. If user has NotificationPanel open, refetch list to show new item. Event is user-scoped — not tied to idea_id.

api_endpoints: WebSocket 'notification' event — user-scoped, triggers toast + bell update
components: useWebSocket hook — add notification event handler

userStories.title: EmailPreferencesPanel Component
userStories.description: As a user, I want to configure my email notification preferences via a floating panel from the user dropdown, so that I can control which events send me emails
acceptanceCriteria: Accessible from user dropdown menu (navbar)
Floating panel with grouped toggles (shadcn Switch)
Groups: Collaboration, Review, Chat, Similarity (all users), Review Management (reviewers), System (admins)
Group toggle switches all items on/off, shows indeterminate state when mixed
Individual toggles per notification type within each group
Saves changes via PATCH /api/users/me/notification-preferences
Shows success toast on save
Role-based visibility: Admin section only visible to admin users
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § PATCH /api/users/me/notification-preferences updates preferences.
Design: docs/03-design/component-inventory.md § EmailPreferencesPanel — grouped toggles, role-based sections. docs/03-design/component-specs.md § 4.4 Switch — toggle styling, 4.5 Checkbox — indeterminate state for group toggles. docs/01-requirements/features.md § FA-13 — role-based notification groups table.
Testing: No test matrix entry — manual verification.
Files: frontend/src/components/notifications/EmailPreferencesPanel.tsx (new), frontend/src/components/layout/Navbar.tsx (add link to user dropdown), frontend/src/api/notifications.ts (add fetchEmailPreferences, updateEmailPreferences), frontend/src/__tests__/email-preferences-panel.test.tsx (new)
Gotchas: Grouped toggles pattern: parent checkbox controls all children, indeterminate when some enabled. Use shadcn Dialog or Popover for floating panel. Notification type mapping from features.md FA-13.3 table. Role-based sections: check user.roles array for 'reviewer' and 'admin'. Save uses PATCH with partial object (only changed preferences). TanStack Query mutation + invalidation.

components: EmailPreferencesPanel — grouped toggles, Dialog/Popover floating window
